title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: FocusGroup separates moderated discussion agents, turn-taking rule, group object. separation lets researcher change moderator guide, participant source, speaker-selection rule retaining one log grammar analysis. FGAgent represents participant moderator generates agent’s turns. model configuration stored config field. ConversationFlow extension base selecting next participant. FocusGroup advances moderator guide records message. LLMR supplies model configurations provider calls.

title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: run_focus_group() single high-level session runner. requires explicit config, constructs roster flow, performs call-count preflight, runs guide, returns focus_group_result. main argument grammar consistent package entry points: n_participants participant count, excluding moderator. guide named vector list phase counts moderator scripts. flow selects speaker-selection rule. message_mode selects role-flipped flat message construction. config model configuration, admin_config group-level configuration direct FocusGroup construction. .runner, supplied, last execution seam. package supplies three built-flow modes: "round_robin", "probabilistic", "desire_based". Construct create_conversation_flow(). implementation classes internal. ConversationFlow remains public packages researchers can define new selection rules. create_agents(), create_agents_from_data(), create_agents_from_survey() return agent-ID-keyed lists include moderator MOD. requires explicit config returned agents can generate model output. focus_group_from_transcript() creates FocusGroup existing transcript without generating new turns. moderator_id identifies moderator known. omitted, importer falls back finding "mod" within speaker identifier.

title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: .runner seam experiments-frame contract used across LLMR family. package calls .runner(experiments, ...), experiments data frame config messages list-columns. runner returns rows least response_text; may also return response_id, token counts, success, related diagnostics. seam handles moderator, participant, desire-scoring, summary requests. contract permits archive replay runner execute full session without live provider. configuration remains explicit part experiment record provenance.

title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: FocusGroup$run_simulation() first records participant roster System message. reads question_script order. entry, moderator generates one message. Question phases call select_next_speaker() collect max_participant_responses participant messages. Opening closing phases collect participant responses. round-robin mode cycles participants. probabilistic mode samples speaking propensities. desire-based mode asks eligible participants model-produced scores selects among eligible candidates. Provider failures desire scoring recorded message metadata trigger one message user. Selection uses neutral draw among candidates; failure never recorded score zero. loop stops script exhausted, num_rounds reached, last closing entry processed. stores final summary final_summary. summary appended conversation_log. FocusGroup object runs . Calling run_simulation() object already run raises error. Construct new object another full session. Use continuation experiment run_focus_studio() compare next message original edited histories.

title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: default message_mode = "roleflip" presents agent’s prior messages assistant messages labels speakers’ messages user messages. message_mode = "flat" places transcript one user message. selected mode recorded session. get_default_prompt_templates() returns templates supported package operations. Pass named replacements prompt_templates FocusGroup$new(). custom participant template containing {{conversation_history}} {{persona_description}} uses flat message path.

title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: conversation_log ordered list message records. record unique integer message_id, moderator cycle round, phase. Moderator participant messages responding question share round. Response identifiers, finish reasons, token counts, provider model labels, duration, failure metadata accompany generated messages. run_focus_group() exposes log transcript data frame result. focus_group_result always fields: focus_group: underlying R6 object. transcript: one row per logged message. summary: final model summary. participants: data frame describing roster. usage: token use LLMR’s sent, rec, total vocabulary. metadata: topic, purpose, flow, message_mode, n_participants, estimated_calls, provider, model, credentials. class concise print method. FocusGroup also concise S3 print method direct R6 objects.

title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: FocusGroup$analyze() descriptive text methods operate stored log. analyze_focus_group() collects principal analyses focus_group_analysis fixed fields: basic_stats, topics, tfidf, readability, themes, model_summary, plots, issues. Missing optional packages unsuitable data produce typed empty components explanatory row issues. plots empty list ggplot2 unavailable. Descriptive analyses run offline. Thematic analysis model summaries run analyze_focus_group() receives explicit config. requested model analysis encounters provider failure raises provider condition.

title: FocusGroup Architecture
text: add turn-taking rule, subclass ConversationFlow implement select_next_speaker(). Override update_state_post_selection() rule keeps state. extension can construct class directly; built-modes remain available create_conversation_flow(). See function class help pages argument defaults return values.

title: Focus Group Analysis
text: FocusGroup stores moderated discussion FocusGroup object. participant moderator FGAgent. group object contains moderator guide, conversation flow, messages, response metadata, analysis methods. Provider calls use LLMR. live session requires provider key usual environment variable. model configuration must passed explicitly.

title: Focus Group Analysis
text: returned focus_group_result six stable fields: focus_group, transcript, summary, participants, usage, metadata. transcript row unique message_id. round identifies moderator cycle, moderator question participant responses share round. phase retains guide phase. Generated rows also carry provider response metadata token counts.
code: config <- LLMR::llm_config(   provider = "openai",   model = "gpt-4o-mini",   temperature = 0.7 )  result <- run_focus_group(   topic = "Impact of Social Media on Mental Health",   n_participants = 5,   guide = c(     Opening = 1,     Icebreaker = 1,     Engagement = 2,     Exploration = 3,     Closing = 1   ),   flow = "desire_based",   config = config,   seed = 110,   message_mode = "roleflip" )  print(result) print(result$focus_group) head(result$transcript) result$participants result$usage

title: Focus Group Analysis
text: analyze_focus_group() runs descriptive analyses without model unless caller opts supplying config. focus_group_analysis fields fixed: basic_stats, topics, tfidf, readability, themes, model_summary, plots, issues. component computed typed empty value. example, plots empty list ggplot2 unavailable. issues identifies optional analysis run gives reason. Individual descriptive analyses remain available methods underlying FocusGroup object:
code: analysis <- analyze_focus_group(   result,   num_topics = 4,   include_plots = TRUE )  print(analysis) analysis$basic_stats analysis$tfidf analysis$readability analysis$issues readability <- result$focus_group$analyze_readability() participation <- result$focus_group$analyze_participation_balance() questions <- result$focus_group$analyze_question_patterns()  readability participation$participation_stats questions$question_patterns

title: Focus Group Analysis
text: Thematic analysis model summaries run explicit config passed analyze_focus_group(). requested model analysis fails, provider condition raised. failure converted missing field omitted result.
code: model_analysis <- analyze_focus_group(   result,   num_topics = 4,   config = config )  cat(model_analysis$themes) cat(model_analysis$model_summary)

what: Import and analyze an existing transcript
title: Focus Group Analysis
text: Importing transcript creates model output. Pass moderator_id moderator known. omitted, importer uses documented substring fallback speaker identifiers.
code: transcript <- data.frame(   speaker = c("Facilitator", "Ana", "Ben", "Ana"),   text = c(     "What should change about the library hours?",     "Evening hours help working parents.",     "Morning crowding has become difficult.",     "Both schedules need enough staff."   ) )  imported <- focus_group_from_transcript(   transcript,   topic = "Library hours",   moderator_id = "Facilitator" )  imported_analysis <- analyze_focus_group(imported, include_plots = FALSE) print(imported_analysis)

title: Focus Group Analysis
text: R6 interface exposes parts assembled run_focus_group(). Built-flows available create_conversation_flow(). ConversationFlow remains public extension base custom flow.
code: agents <- create_agents(   n_participants = 3,   demographics = data.frame(     age = c(25, 35, 45),     gender = c("male", "female", "non-binary"),     education = c("high school", "bachelor's", "master's"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   config = config )  flow <- create_conversation_flow(   mode = "desire_based",   agents = agents,   moderator_id = "MOD" )  fg <- FocusGroup$new(   topic = "Impact of Social Media on Mental Health",   purpose = "Explore perspectives and experiences related to social media.",   agents = agents,   moderator_id = "MOD",   turn_taking_flow = flow,   admin_config = config )

title: Focus Group Analysis
text: generated transcript evidence specified simulation, population. Analysis retain guide, participant records, model configuration, message construction recorded result.

title: FocusGroup
text: FocusGroup runs moderated discussion language models act moderator participants. moderator guide determines moderator , conversation flow selects participant. package records message, phase round, response metadata, token use FocusGroup object. Model requests use LLMR. package intended piloting moderator guides discussion protocols fieldwork. generated transcript records model responses specified guide, persona set, model configuration. estimate public opinion. principal public objects : run_focus_group(), constructs runs complete session. FGAgent, represents one participant moderator stores config. FocusGroup, owns roster, guide, flow, prompts, session log. ConversationFlow, extension base class speaker-selection rules. create_agents(), create_agents_from_data(), create_agents_from_survey(), construct agents supplied personas respondent records. create_conversation_flow(), constructs built-flow mode name. focus_group_from_transcript(), imports existing transcript. analyze_focus_group(), runs main transcript analyses. run_focus_studio(), starts optional Shiny interface. FocusGroup experimental. public interface may change stable release.

title: FocusGroup
text: Text analysis plotting use optional packages: Set provider key environment variable making live provider call. OpenAI model, LLMR reads OPENAI_API_KEY.
code: install.packages("LLMR") remotes::install_github("asanaei/FocusGroup") install.packages(c("ggplot2", "quanteda", "quanteda.textstats",                    "topicmodels", "tidytext"))

title: FocusGroup
text: Every operation generates model output requires explicit config. guide accepts phase counts ordered moderator instructions. Supported phase names Opening, Icebreaker, Engagement, Exploration, Closing. run_focus_group() returns focus_group_result stable fields: focus_group, transcript, summary, participants, usage, metadata. participants data frame. usage uses LLMR’s sent, rec, total token vocabulary. metadata contains topic, purpose, flow, message_mode, n_participants, estimated_calls, provider, model. Credentials never included. live run, function estimates number model calls. request max_calls stops unless confirm = TRUE. Retries incomplete outputs can increase realized call count. Supplying .runner bypasses live cost stop. FocusGroup object represents one session runs . Construct another object another full session. continuation experiment run_focus_studio() documented path comparing generated continuations existing session.
code: library(FocusGroup) library(LLMR)  config <- LLMR::llm_config(   provider = "openai",   model = "gpt-4o-mini",   temperature = 0.7,   max_tokens = 200 )  moderator_guide <- list(   Opening = "Welcome the group and state the ground rules.",   Icebreaker = c(     "What first comes to mind when you think about remote work?",     "What experience has shaped that impression?"   ),   Engagement = "Where does remote work enter your daily routine?",   Exploration = c(     "What tensions arise between flexibility and coordination?",     "Whose perspective has received too little attention?"   ),   Closing = "Thank the participants and close the session." )  result <- run_focus_group(   topic = "The impact of remote work on team collaboration",   n_participants = 4,   guide = moderator_guide,   flow = "desire_based",   config = config,   seed = 110,   message_mode = "roleflip",   verbose = TRUE )  print(result) head(result$transcript, 5) result$usage cat(result$summary)

what: Run a session without a provider
title: FocusGroup
text: .runner seam uses experiments-frame contract LLMRcontent archive replay. receives data frame config messages list-columns returns rows least response_text. explicit configuration remains required part experiment row.
code: scripted_runner <- function(experiments, ...) {   experiments$response_text <- vapply(experiments$messages, function(messages) {     prompt <- paste(vapply(messages, `[[`, character(1), "content"),                     collapse = "\n")     if (grepl("Desire to talk score", prompt, fixed = TRUE)) {       return("8")     }     if (grepl("Summary:", prompt, fixed = TRUE)) {       return("The session recorded distinct positions and a disagreement.")     }     paste(       "This scripted response states one position on library funding and gives",       "a concrete reason that another participant could examine."     )   }, character(1))   experiments$success <- TRUE   experiments }  offline_config <- LLMR::llm_config("openai", "gpt-4o-mini")  offline_result <- run_focus_group(   topic = "Library funding priorities",   n_participants = 2,   guide = list(     Opening = "Welcome the group.",     Engagement = "Which funding priority matters most?",     Closing = "Thank the participants and close the session."   ),   flow = "round_robin",   config = offline_config,   max_participant_responses = 1,   verbose = FALSE,   .runner = scripted_runner )  offline_result$focus_group$analyze()$speaker_stats

title: FocusGroup
text: Built-turn-taking implementations constructed create_conversation_flow(). ConversationFlow remains public new flow classes can inherit . FGAgent$new() also takes config; public configuration field name. Public model-dependent methods use config, caller-controlled execution uses .runner final ordinary seam.
code: agents <- create_agents(   n_participants = 3,   config = config )  session_flow <- create_conversation_flow(   mode = "round_robin",   agents = agents,   moderator_id = "MOD" )  fg <- FocusGroup$new(   topic = "Weekend preferences and activities",   purpose = "Describe how participants prefer to spend their weekends.",   agents = agents,   moderator_id = "MOD",   turn_taking_flow = session_flow,   question_script = list(     list(phase = "opening"),     list(       phase = "exploration_question",       text = "What makes a weekend relaxing or fulfilling for you?"     ),     list(phase = "closing")   ),   admin_config = config )  fg$run_simulation(verbose = TRUE)

code: data(anes_2024_personas, package = "LLMR")  panel_agents <- create_agents_from_data(   anes_2024_personas,   n_participants = 6,   config = config )

title: ConversationFlow Base Class — ConversationFlow
text: `ConversationFlow` R6 base class defining interface turn-taking mechanisms focus group simulation. Subclasses implement specific strategies selecting next speaker.

title: ConversationFlow Base Class — ConversationFlow
text: `initialize(agents, moderator_id)` Sets flow. Call `super$initialize(agents, moderator_id)`. `select_next_speaker(focus_group)` *Required*. Logic choose next `FGAgent` speak.     return agent object `NULL`. `update_state_post_selection(speaker_id, focus_group)` Optional. Updates internal state     speaker spoken. Base implementation updates `self$last_speaker_id`.

title: ConversationFlow Base Class — ConversationFlow
text: agents named list `FGAgent` objects participating conversation. participant_ids character vector agent identifiers, excluding moderator. moderator_id Character. ID moderator agent. last_speaker_id ID agent last spoke. Can `NULL`. selection_metadata list provenance latest selection. Desire-scoring failures record condition neutral fallback .

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
text: FGAgent Class FGAgent Class

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
text: Represents agent (participant moderator) focus group simulation. agent unique ID, persona, LLM configuration, methods generate utterances express desire speak.

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
text: `FGAgent` designed flexible: **Per-Agent LLM Configuration**: agent initialized     `llm_config` (stored `config` field). allows different     agents use different LLMs, temperatures, even providers. **Persona Definition**: `agent_details` (containing `demographics`,     `survey_responses`, `direct_persona_description`, /`communication_style`)     used construct `persona_description` `communication_style_instruction`. **Subclassing**: advanced customization, inherit `FGAgent` override     methods add new fields.

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
text: id Character. Unique identifier agent. persona_description Character. Textual description agent's persona, derived demographics, survey responses, direct input. communication_style_instruction Character. specific instruction agent's communication style, included prompts. config `llm_config` object (`LLMR` package) specifying LLM provider, model, API key, parameters agent. .runner `NULL` experiments-frame runner. receives data frame `config` `messages` list-columns returns rows least `response_text`. is_moderator Logical. `TRUE` agent moderator, `FALSE` otherwise. history List. log utterances made agent simulation. tokens_sent_agent Numeric. Total tokens sent agent. tokens_received_agent Numeric. Total tokens received agent. role Character. "moderator" "participant" convenience reports. demographics Named list. Raw demographics used build persona. survey_responses Named list. Raw survey responses used build persona.

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
text: FGAgent$new() FGAgent$generate_utterance() FGAgent$get_need_to_talk() FGAgent$clone()

text: Initialize new FGAgent.

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
code: FGAgent$new(id, agent_details, config, is_moderator = FALSE, .runner = NULL)

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
text: id Character. unique identifier agent. agent_details List. Contains information build agent's persona. Can include: `demographics`: named list demographic attributes (e.g., `list(age = 30, occupation = "teacher")`). `survey_responses`: named list survey questions answers. `direct_persona_description`: character string used directly persona. Overrides demographics/survey provided. `communication_style`: character string describing agent's communication style (e.g., "analytical direct", "empathetic story-driven"). `is_moderator` `TRUE` specific details provided, default moderator persona used. config `llm_config` object `LLMR::llm_config()`. may `NULL` analysis-agent generate output. is_moderator Logical. `TRUE` agent moderator, `FALSE` otherwise. .runner `NULL` experiments-frame runner. `NULL` uses live LLMR calls.

title: FGAgent Class — FGAgent
text: topic Character. current discussion topic. conversation_history_string Character. Formatted string recent conversation history. utterance_prompt_template Character. prompt template use. max_tokens_utterance Integer. Maximum tokens generated utterance. current_moderator_question Character. current question posed moderator. conversation_summary_so_far Character. summary earlier parts conversation. current_phase Character. current phase focus group (e.g., "icebreaker", "exploration"). conversation_log List NULL. structured conversation log. supplied (template legacy flat template), message built role-flipped: agent's prior turns become `assistant` messages others' become labeled `user` messages. `NULL` keeps legacy flat single-user-message construction. standing_rules Character NULL. system-message standing rules (persona-anchoring, safety, etc.) role-flipped path; `NULL` uses built-default appropriate agent's role. self_state Logical. `TRUE` (default), compact "points already made" digest agent's prior turns added system message (participants ) discourage self-repetition.

title: FocusGroup: Simulated Moderated Discussions — FocusGroup-package
text: FocusGroup simulates moderated discussions language model participants. [FocusGroup] object advances moderator script, selects participant speakers, stores message structured conversation log. can also hold imported transcript analysis.

title: FocusGroup: Simulated Moderated Discussions — FocusGroup-package
text: [run_focus_group()] accepts phase counts ordered moderator instructions returns structured `focus_group_result`. direct construction, create [FGAgent] objects [ConversationFlow], pass [FocusGroup]. Built-flows constructed [create_conversation_flow()]. [create_agents()], [create_agents_from_data()], [create_agents_from_survey()] construct agents direct personas respondent records. [focus_group_from_transcript()] imports existing transcript without generating new turns. Model calls require explicit `config` built [LLMR::llm_config()]. high-level runner agent constructors accept `.runner` function using experiments-frame contract caller-controlled execution. Descriptive analysis imported transcript require provider call. Model summaries thematic analysis opt-explicit `config`.

title: FocusGroup: Simulated Moderated Discussions — FocusGroup-package
text: Ali Sanaei sanaei@uchicago.edu

title: FocusGroup R6 Class — FocusGroup
text: Main R6 class managing running focus group simulation. orchestrates simulation distinct phases (Opening, Icebreaker, Engagement, Exploration, Closing) guided question script, manages agent interactions via turn-taking flow, provides methods analysis visualization conversation.

title: FocusGroup R6 Class — FocusGroup
text: topic Character. main topic focus group discussion. purpose Character. primary purpose research objective conducting focus group. agents Named list. list `FGAgent` objects participating simulation, indexed IDs. moderator_id Character. ID agent designated moderator. conversation_log List. chronological log messages. message list includes least `message_id`, `round`, `speaker_id`, `is_moderator`, `text`, `timestamp`, `phase`, `metadata`, along call metadata: `response_id`, `finish_reason`, `sent_tokens`, `rec_tokens`, `total_tokens`, `duration_s`, `provider`, `model`. `message_id` unique message order; moderator participant messages one moderator cycle share `round`. turn_taking_flow `ConversationFlow` object dictating participant turn-taking. prompt_templates List. Holds prompt templates agent/moderator actions. question_script List. structured list defining phases specific questions/actions moderator. element list `phase` (e.g., "opening", "icebreaker_question") optionally `text` (specific questions). current_question_text Character. Text current question discussed. current_conversation_summary Character. LLM-generated summary earlier parts conversation, used managing context length prompts. final_summary Character. Final LLM-generated summary recent simulation run. message_mode Character. message construction used recent `run_simulation()` ("roleflip" "flat"), recorded saved object can replayed (e.g. GUI continuation experiment) construction. admin_config `llm_config` object group-level model tasks. max_tokens_utterance Integer. Default max tokens participant utterances. max_tokens_moderator Integer. Default max tokens moderator utterances. max_tokens_desire Integer. Default max tokens desire--talk queries. max_participant_responses Integer. Maximum number participant exchanges per round moderator can intervene. Can also set globally via `options(focusgroup.max_participant_responses = N)`. total_tokens_sent Numeric. Total tokens sent across LLM calls group. total_tokens_received Numeric. Total tokens received across LLM calls.

title: FocusGroup R6 Class — FocusGroup
text: FocusGroup$new() FocusGroup$run_simulation() FocusGroup$summarize() FocusGroup$analyze() FocusGroup$analyze_topics() FocusGroup$analyze_tfidf() FocusGroup$analyze_readability() FocusGroup$analyze_themes() FocusGroup$analyze_statistics() FocusGroup$analyze_participation_balance() FocusGroup$analyze_response_patterns() FocusGroup$analyze_question_patterns() FocusGroup$analyze_key_phrases() FocusGroup$plot_participation_timeline() FocusGroup$plot_word_count_distribution() FocusGroup$plot_participation_by_agent() FocusGroup$plot_message_length_timeline() FocusGroup$clone()

title: FocusGroup R6 Class — FocusGroup
code: FocusGroup$new(   topic,   purpose,   agents,   moderator_id,   turn_taking_flow,   question_script = list(),   prompt_templates = list(),   admin_config = NULL,   max_tokens_config = list(),   max_participant_responses = NULL )

title: FocusGroup R6 Class — FocusGroup
text: topic Character. main discussion topic. purpose Character. primary purpose focus group. agents Named list initialized `FGAgent` objects. moderator_id Character. ID agent acting moderator. turn_taking_flow initialized `ConversationFlow` object. question_script List. Moderator's script defining phases questions. empty, minimal default script (opening, generic discussion, closing) used. prompt_templates List. Custom prompt templates. Defaults used provided. admin_config `llm_config` object administrative model tasks. `NULL`, moderator's explicitly supplied `config` used. max_tokens_config List. Optional. Named list `utterance`, `moderator`, `desire` override default max token limits LLM call types. max_participant_responses Integer. Optional. Maximum participant exchanges per round moderator intervention. Defaults `getOption("focusgroup.max_participant_responses", 3)`.

title: FocusGroup R6 Class — FocusGroup
text: Run full focus group simulation. Iterates `question_script` phases specified number rounds.

title: FocusGroup R6 Class — FocusGroup

what: Create agents from a labeled survey file — create_agents_from_survey
title: Create agents from a labeled survey file — create_agents_from_survey
text: Reads labeled survey file (Stata `.dta`, SPSS `.sav`, SAS `.sas7bdat`), decodes chosen variables value labels, turns selected respondent `FGAgent` whose persona states respondent's demographics survey answers. Numeric codes decoded file's value labels, call works ANES, GSS, WVS, labeled file; nothing particular dataset hard-coded.

title: Create agents from a labeled survey file — create_agents_from_survey
code: create_agents_from_survey(   n_participants,   survey_path,   config,   demographic_vars = NULL,   survey_vars = NULL,   rows = NULL,   weights = NULL,   na_strings = .fg_default_na_strings,   .runner = NULL )

title: Create agents from a labeled survey file — create_agents_from_survey
text: n_participants Integer number participants (excludes moderator). survey_path Path survey file (`.dta`, `.sav`, `.sas7bdat`). config explicit `LLMR::llm_config` agents. demographic_vars Variable names (codes) render demographics. May named vector, case names shown field labels. `NULL`, common demographic variables auto-detected labels. survey_vars Variable names (codes) render survey responses. May named (names become question wording shown). `NULL`, labeled variables demographics used. rows Optional row selector restricting eligible respondents sampling: integer logical vector, predicate `function(df) -> logical` decoded demographics frame. weights Optional sampling weights: column name file, numeric vector aligned file's rows. Used weight respondents drawn. na_strings Character vector value-label substrings treated missing (case-insensitive). Defaults small common set; pass match another file's missing-data vocabulary. .runner Optional experiments-frame runner stored every agent.

title: Create agents from a labeled survey file — create_agents_from_survey
text: named list `FGAgent` objects (participants + moderator), keyed   agent ID.

title: Create agents from a labeled survey file — create_agents_from_survey
text: survey answers keyed variable's question wording (label file) model sees item, code name. persona draws inferences answers; states lets model interpret.

title: Create agents from a labeled survey file — create_agents_from_survey

Default "text". topic Character `NULL`. discussion topic, used model-dependent analyses stored object. `NULL` falls back "Imported transcript". moderator_id Character `NULL`. Exact speaker id treat moderator. `NULL`, documented "mod" substring fallback used.

title: Build a FocusGroup object from an imported transcript — focus_group_from_transcript
text: One lightweight agent created per speaker speaker-aware methods roster report ; agents carry minimal placeholder persona meant continue conversation. Set `moderator_id` transcript identifies moderator. `NULL`, moderator first speaker whose id contains "mod" (case-insensitive), e.g. "Moderator" "MOD". fallback finds match, non-speaking "MOD" agent added object complete, appears `analyze()` zero utterances. Rows whose speaker missing blank dropped warning; rows speaker "System" kept log excluded analyses, matching simulator treats roster message.

title: Build a FocusGroup object from an imported transcript — focus_group_from_transcript
code: transcript <- data.frame(   speaker = c("Moderator", "Ana", "Ben", "Ana", "Moderator", "Ben"),   text = c(     "Welcome, everyone. What do you make of the new library hours?",     "They help working parents like me; evenings matter most.",     "I see it differently. Mornings are now far too crowded.",     "Crowded mornings still beat being locked out after work.",     "Ben, say more about what changed in the mornings.",     "Staff are stretched thin before noon, so lines are longer."   ) ) fg <- focus_group_from_transcript(transcript, topic = "library hours") fg$analyze_participation_balance()$participation_stats #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   speaker_id messages words avg_words message_percentage word_percentage #>   <chr>         <int> <int>     <dbl>              <dbl>           <dbl> #> 1 Ana               2    18         9               33.3            31.0 #> 2 Ben               2    20        10               33.3            34.5 #> 3 Moderator         2    20        10               33.3            34.5

title: Get Default Prompt Templates — get_default_prompt_templates
text: Retrieves list containing default prompt templates used `FocusGroup` package. templates guide behavior LLM agents (participants moderator) different phases actions within simulation.

title: Get Default Prompt Templates — get_default_prompt_templates
code: get_default_prompt_templates()

title: Get Default Prompt Templates — get_default_prompt_templates
text: named list element character string representing prompt template.         Placeholders like `{{topic}}`, `{{persona_description}}`, etc., used within         templates filled dynamically simulation.

title: Get Default Prompt Templates — get_default_prompt_templates
text: Users can retrieve list, modify specific templates, pass modified list `FocusGroup$new()` higher-level wrapper functions customize simulation's prompts.

title: Get Default Prompt Templates — get_default_prompt_templates
text: Two participant templates ship , one used depends message construction (see `message_mode` argument [run_focus_group()], `options(focusgroup.message_mode=)`). `participant_turn_instruction` canonical template     default role-flipped construction. carries current question     turn cue, persona, standing rules, transcript     supplied structurally (system message plus role-separated turns). `participant_utterance_subtle_persona` flat template.     inlines persona whole     transcript one user message. custom template contains     `{{conversation_history}}` `{{persona_description}}` treated     flat template routed flat path.

title: Get Default Prompt Templates — get_default_prompt_templates
code:
default_prompts <- get_default_prompt_templates() print(names(default_prompts)) #>  [1] "participant_utterance_subtle_persona" #>  [2] "participant_turn_instruction"         #>  [3] "participant_desire_instruction"       #>  [4] "participant_desire_to_talk_nuanced"   #>  [5] "moderator_opening"                    #>  [6] "moderator_icebreaker_question"        #>  [7] "moderator_engagement_question"        #>  [8] "moderator_exploration_question"       #>  [9] "moderator_probing_focused"            #> [10] "moderator_summarizing"                #> [11] "moderator_transition"                 #> [12] "moderator_manage_participation"       #> [13] "moderator_ending_question"            #> [14] "moderator_closing"                    #> [15] "moderator_generic_utterance"          #> [16] "thematic_analysis_prompt"             print(default_prompts$participant_utterance_subtle_persona) #> [1] "You are a focus group participant.
Your defining characteristics, lived experiences, priorities, and any prior survey responses are:
{{persona_description}}
{{communication_style_instruction}}

The main topic of this focus group is: {{topic}}.
The current question or point of discussion, initiated by the moderator, is: '{{current_moderator_question}}'.
Summary of earlier discussion (if available):
{{conversation_summary_so_far}}
Most recent messages in the conversation:
{{conversation_history}}

Instructions for your response (persona-anchored and safe to disagree):
1.  Internalize and embody the persona above. Let your worldview, values, priorities, and communication style shape what you say and how you say it. Sound like a distinct person, not a generic commentator.
2.  Do NOT write things like 'As a [role]...' or 'My persona is...'. Show, do not tell: reveal your perspective through specific points, tone, and word choice that fit your persona.
3.  Advance the conversation: respond directly to the current question and to others' points. If you agree, add a new angle or example. If you disagree, do so respectfully: acknowledge their point, then state your different view and give a clear reason or brief example.
4.  Make it concrete. Prefer specific reasons, brief anecdotes, or practical examples over vague generalities. Vary your language and avoid repeating yourself or others verbatim.
5.  Keep it appropriate for a group turn: 2-6 sentences. Aim for one clear claim and one succinct rationale (optionally a short example). Avoid meta-comments about the discussion mechanics.
6.  Psychological safety: it is safe to disagree respectfully. Critique ideas, not people; use civil language; you may address participants by ID when relevant.
7.  Never reveal you are an AI model.

Based on all the above, what is your contribution to the discussion now?"  # To customize a prompt: my_prompts <- get_default_prompt_templates() my_prompts$moderator_opening <- "Welcome all! Let us talk about {{topic}} today." # Then use `my_prompts` when creating a FocusGroup object.

what: Run a Focus Group Simulation — run_focus_group
title: Run a Focus Group Simulation — run_focus_group
text: Constructs agents, moderator guide, turn-taking flow, runs one focus group session. model configuration explicit. live run, function estimates number generated model outputs applies `max_calls` limit unless `confirm` `TRUE`.

title: Run a Focus Group Simulation — run_focus_group
code: run_focus_group(   topic,   config,   n_participants = 6,   guide = c(Opening = 2, Icebreaker = 3, Engagement = 8, Exploration = 10, Closing = 2),   demographics = NULL,   survey_responses = NULL,   flow = "desire_based",   seed = NULL,   message_mode = c("roleflip", "flat"),   verbose = TRUE,   max_participant_responses = NULL,   max_calls = 100L,   confirm = FALSE,   .runner = NULL )

title: Run a Focus Group Simulation — run_focus_group
text: topic Character. Focus group topic. config explicit `LLMR::llm_config` used agents group-level model tasks. n_participants Integer. Number participants, excluding moderator. guide named numeric vector named list. Numeric values select many moderator instructions phase banks. Character vectors supply ordered instructions directly. demographics Optional participant demographics. survey_responses Optional participant survey responses. flow Character. One `"round_robin"`, `"probabilistic"`, `"desire_based"`. seed Optional integer governing -package sampling. message_mode Character. `"roleflip"` `"flat"`. verbose Logical. Print session progress. max_participant_responses Optional integer maximum number participant responses per moderator question. max_calls Integer. Maximum estimated live model outputs allowed without confirmation. confirm Logical. Permit live run whose estimate exceeds `max_calls`. .runner Optional experiments-frame runner. receives data frame `config` `messages` list-columns returns rows least `response_text`.

title: Run a Focus Group Simulation — run_focus_group
text: `focus_group_result` group, transcript, summary,   participant table, token usage, sanitized metadata.

title: Run a Focus Group Simulation — run_focus_group
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cfg <- LLMR::llm_config("openai", "gpt-4o-mini") result <- run_focus_group(   topic = "Library funding priorities",   config = cfg,   n_participants = 4,   guide = list(     Opening = "Welcome the participants and state the ground rules.",     Exploration = "Which funding priority deserves attention first?",     Closing = "Thank the participants and close the session."   ),   flow = "round_robin" ) } # }

what: Launch the FocusGroup Shiny GUI — run_focus_studio
title: Launch the FocusGroup Shiny GUI — run_focus_studio
text: point--click front end three tabs. Run focus group starts fresh moderated session live topic handful participants, shows transcript, offers downloadable .rds. Analyze loads transcript reads participation word statistics offline. continuation experiment takes saved focus group message, perturbs earlier message, generates next message original perturbed histories, downstream message can compared. Running continuation experiment generate text need API key; analysis works offline.

title: Launch the FocusGroup Shiny GUI — run_focus_studio
code: run_focus_studio(...)

title: Launch the FocusGroup Shiny GUI — run_focus_studio
text: ... Passed shiny::runApp().

title: Launch the FocusGroup Shiny GUI — run_focus_studio
text: Invisibly, value shiny::runApp(); called side   effect starting app.

title: Launch the FocusGroup Shiny GUI — run_focus_studio
text: GUI optional. needs suggested packages shiny, bslib, DT, LLMR.shiny; install first. Keys read environment variables , never pasted app.

title: Launch the FocusGroup Shiny GUI — run_focus_studio
code: if (interactive() &&     all(vapply(c("shiny", "bslib", "DT", "LLMR.shiny"),                requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE))) {   run_focus_studio() }

title: FocusGroup 0.5.1
text: Initial CRAN release. run_focus_group() single high-level session runner. requires explicit config, accepts n_participants, guide, flow, message_mode, performs model-call cost preflight live work. run_focus_group() returns focus_group_result stable fields focus_group, transcript, summary, participants, usage, metadata. print method gives concise run summary. create_agents(), create_agents_from_data(), create_agents_from_survey() construct agent-ID-keyed rosters supplied personas, -memory records, labeled survey files, pre-rendered persona panels. ConversationFlow public extension base. Built-round-robin, probabilistic, desire-based flows constructed create_conversation_flow(). Model configuration arguments use config; group-level configuration uses admin_config. FGAgent configuration field config. .runner seam accepts experiments data frame config messages list-columns returns rows least response_text. Session logs give every row unique integer message_id record moderator cycle round, retaining phase. analyze_focus_group() returns focus_group_analysis stable fields, typed empty components, issues table. Descriptive analysis runs offline. Thematic analysis model summaries require explicit config. focus_group_from_transcript() accepts moderator_id; omitted, speaker identifiers containing "mod" provide fallback. get_default_prompt_templates() contains templates used supported package operations. run_focus_studio() starts optional Shiny interface running sessions, reading transcripts, comparing continuations edited histories.
